title: Hello, Spark
excerpt: Why this blog runs on HTML that infers its own backend.
body:
This site is a handful of .html files and one SQLite database.

Each page declares the data it needs in a <spark-ssr> block, and the server infers the rest: routes from filenames, APIs from SQL, auth from a user_id column — even these tables and rows came from seed files the templates point at.

View source on any page — what you see is what I wrote.

title: HTML that reacts
excerpt: Templates, loops and conditionals — in plain HTML attributes.
body:
A <template each="post in posts"> renders a list. A <template if> branches. {curly} braces interpolate.

On the server they render to static HTML; in the browser the same syntax comes alive as components. One mental model, both sides of the wire.

title: Zero-config SSR
excerpt: Filesystem routing, layouts, sessions, live updates — from the template.
body:
pages/blog/[slug].html serves /blog/:slug, and :slug binds straight into the page query. pages/_layout.html wraps every page — nav, styles, footer, once.

Declare <spark-ssr table="todos" live /> and the REST endpoints exist, the table is created from the template, and every open tab stays in sync. Delete the file and it all goes away.

title: Drafts are private
excerpt: This post is unpublished — only the signed-in author sees it.
body:
The page query keeps drafts out of anonymous requests:

WHERE slug = :slug AND (published = 1 OR :session.id IS NOT NULL)

And a missing row is a REAL 404 now — <template else status="404"> sets the response status, so crawlers never index an empty page. Sign in at /admin and this post appears.
